title: New buildkit-mount tests: fail in gating
body:
There's a bunch of new tests in bud.bats, all of them referring to `tests/bud/buildkit-mount`. These are failing in gating:
```
not ok 276 bud-with-mount-like-buildkit
...
# STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/test,z cat /test/input_file
# error building at STEP "RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/test,z cat /test/input_file": error resolving mountpoints for container "57c3e5eb268e14a8b8bca356504082f5869e66133319cbfeb75533d3d4f26c82": relabeling content in /usr is not allowed
# [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
Reason: in gating, tests are installed via RPM into `/usr/share/buildah/tests/system`.

I'm well past my done-for-the-week point, and can't fix this today or tomorrow. I would be oh-so-grateful if someone were to fix this soon, but if not I'll take a stab at it on Monday.

comments.body: @edsantiago Most of these tests were added by me. This looks like `selinux` issue to me but dont know why it passes in our CI and not on gating tests.

> Can we move the gating tests to /var/tmp?

That's a really complex question with many answers that I don't have time for right now. My recommendation is: no test should ever, ever, ever modify anything in its source directory. If a test needs to modify a source file, even something trivial like permission or SELinux context, it should work on a copy. Conveniently, the test framework provides `$TESTDIR` (not to be confused with the plural one) which is a guaranteed-empty tmpdir for each subtest to play with.
@edsantiago I agree with you, I'll rectify these tests to do testing in a tmp dir.
I think above PR would close the issue.
